title: Decoder output-projection producer cadence sensitivity
hypothesis: The promoted serial_lpc1 ranker is safe for the current streaming-weight output-projection producer model, but resident or cache-backed output weights may make producer tiles arrive faster than serial_lpc1 can absorb without buffering.

direct_comparison.primary_question: At what output-projection weight residency/cache-hit assumptions does the producer II cross the replay-observed zero-backpressure thresholds for serial_lpc1, lpc2, and lpc4?
include: streaming-weight through resident-weight cache hit rates
replay-observed zero-backpressure thresholds instead of only analytical ranker service cycles

expected_benefit: separates ranker promotion under streaming weights from the faster resident-weight producer case
identifies when lpc1 remains valid and when lpc2/lpc4 or rank-tree/buffering should be evaluated
sets up measured producer trace work with explicit cadence risk boundaries
risks: cache hit rate is a planning parameter, not measured hardware behavior
bursty producer traffic with buffering may differ from fixed-II assumptions
wider producer tiles require separate banking assumptions

required_evaluations.objective: Estimate output-projection producer II under streaming, partially cached, and resident weights, then compare against serial ranker replay thresholds.

expected_result.reason: If resident/cache-backed producer rows are unsafe for all measured serial rankers, plan a buffered or rank-tree producer-coupled wrapper; otherwise select the lowest-power serial guard point for that cadence.
